chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-3339 of 2021 ______________________________________________________________ Date Order With Signature Of Judge ______________________________________________________________ 1.For order on office objection 2.For hg of CMA No.14067/23 3.For hg of main case 25.02.2025. Syed Sultan Ahmed, advocate for petitioner. Mr. Muhammad Hisham Mahar, Assistant Attorney General. Mr. Gulfaraz Khattak, Assistant Attorney General. -------------- MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO, J:- We have heard learned counsel for petitioner. Petitioner claims that in a public auction she was granted a Plot No.SR-5, measuring 248.76 sq.yds., Sector 5-C, KDA Scheme No.41, Surjani Town, Karachi vide bid letter dated 04.07.2005 against the cost of Rs.3500/- per sq. yard. She, in compliance of bid letter, submitted the cost of the plot through different challans, which are available from page 19 to 25 of the file. However, in spite of paying the entire cost and completing other formalities, possession of the plot was not given to the petitioner. Petitioner then moved so many applications to the KDA/respondent No. 3 but to no avail. Finally, vide letter dated 09.03.2018 in lieu of subject plot, petitioner was allotted Plot No.33, Sector 7-A in the same scheme. However, up till now even this letter has not been enforced by the KDA and the possession of the plot has not been handed over to the petitioner. 2. We have seen that this petition is pending since May 2021 and KDA has been offered many opportunities to file comments in rebuttal but in vain. Today also no one is present on behalf of the KDA. Apparently from the record available in the file, the claim of the petitioner appears to be valid.

Today also no one is present on behalf of the KDA. Apparently from the record available in the file, the claim of the petitioner appears to be valid. We, therefore, direct respondent No. 2 to look into the matter of the petitioner, resolve her issue in accordance with law and if the allotment order dated 09.03.2018 is genuine, shall enforce it within a period of two months. After two months, compliance report shall be filed through MIT-II for a perusal in Chamber. This petition, in terms as above, is disposed of. JUDGE HANIF JUDGE
